resources.string: No problem — you can explore the demo to see how Hermes-Relay works, and connect your own Hermes server anytime from Settings → Connections. Relay pairing for power tools can be added later too.
Chat with Hermes and manage your dashboard from your phone.
Connect to your running Hermes dashboard and API. No Relay install or pairing required.
Add Hermes-Relay for Terminal, Bridge, relay sessions, and channel grants.
The setup guide has copy/paste commands when you need to start Hermes on a computer or server.
Your Hermes agent, streaming in real time.
Live responses with tool progress, markdown, and rich cards as the agent works.
Switch agent profiles mid-flow — each keeps its own sessions, model, and persona.
Tap the mic to talk. Speech runs through your Hermes server — no extra install.
Profiles, skills, automations, MCP servers, models, and provider keys.
Search the hub, read a skill before installing, and install from your phone.
Signing into the dashboard once also unlocks voice for this connection.
Pair the optional Relay when you want more than standard.
A real tmux session on your server, in your pocket.
Let the agent operate this phone — with safety rails (sideload builds).
Provider-native realtime voice agent and profile-aware voice providers.
Open a server shell through your paired relay session.
Let Hermes send approved bridge commands to this phone.
Can\'t edit right now — wait for the current turn to finish
Voice needs a one-time sign-in on the %1$s route — open Manage
Voice needs dashboard sign-in — open Manage to sign in
This Hermes build has no voice routes — update hermes-agent or pair Relay
Voice needs a reachable Hermes dashboard or Relay voice route
Connect your Hermes server to start chatting — or explore a quick demo first. You can connect anytime.
Tap Open Settings and grant Microphone access to use voice mode.
Hold the chat for a clean, focused view
Enable Display over other apps, then return to start Voice Overlay.
Voice Overlay could not be started.
Voice Overlay permission was not granted.
Camera permission needed to take a photo
Vanilla Hermes voice needs dashboard sign-in
Add a Vanilla Hermes API/dashboard connection
Waiting for Android to report an internet route
Chat and profile calls may not be available
No paired session or pending pair code
Vanilla Hermes voice uses the host\'s global TTS
The active profile’s conversation history could not be reached. Reconnect and try again.
Chat stream dropped — recovering the answer in the background
Start the Hermes API/dashboard on your host, then connect this app. Relay pairing is optional and only needed for Terminal, Bridge, relay sessions, and channel grants.
Terminal, Bridge, Relay sessions, and grants require the Relay plugin.
Skip for now — set up later in Settings

Use this for Chat and Manage. Pair Relay later only when you enable Terminal, Bridge, Relay sessions, or grants. Dashboard sign-in is the preferred upstream auth path; the API key remains the Android Chat fallback.
Needed for Android Chat until the dashboard gateway transport is enabled.
Routes: default first, Tailscale fallback when reachable.
The scan uses these ports. The API server URL above can also use any custom port.
You already have a connection to this server:
Update this connection instead of creating another card. Only one connection per server is kept.
API/dashboard setup for Chat, Manage, Skills, Cron, MCP, Profiles, Models, and Settings
Scan a QR with API URL/key for Hermes; Relay QR details require the Relay plugin
Power-user path for Terminal, Bridge, Relay sessions, and grants
No camera or QR? Register this phone\'s code on the host
Hermes API used by Chat and sessions — API port 8642 and http:// assumed for bare hosts (the dashboard\'s 9119 is derived separately)
Scanning this LAN for Hermes dashboard/API…
Sign in via the dashboard to unlock Manage and voice — the API key is optional for Chat.
Already running the Relay plugin? Pair here to enable Terminal, Bridge, and channel grants.
Opens the dashboard web UI to complete sign-in.
Leave blank for the standard dashboard port on the API host
Use this only after the host has registered a Relay pairing code. Standard Chat and Manage do not require this.
Hermes API — chat and sessions (default port 8642)
Hermes-Relay — Terminal, Bridge, relay sessions, and grants
Use this when you can\'t scan a Relay pairing QR. Set your URLs, then register the code below on the host running Hermes-Relay.
2. On the host running Hermes-Relay, run:
3. Then come back and tap Connect
Your phone tries these routes in order and uses the first one it can reach. It switches automatically as you change networks.
This relay uses plain ws:// — traffic is not encrypted.
Only continue if you trust the network on any connection (LAN, Tailscale, VPN).
I understand this pairing sends traffic in plain text — visible to anyone on the network.
%1$s is plain ws:// — fine at home or the office, not on public Wi-Fi.
%1$s is encrypted (wss://) and the app uses it automatically when %2$s is unreachable.
Checking the API server, saved key, and dashboard.
Dashboard will be checked in Manage
Speech ready via your Hermes server
Hermes build has no voice routes — update or pair Relay
Checked once the dashboard is reachable
Fallback route ready for use away from home
LAN only — add a Tailscale or public route in Settings → Connections → Routes
The host hasn\'t accepted this pairing code yet. Run the pairing command on your Hermes host (or re-check the code), then tap Retry.

Timed out before the relay confirmed pairing. Check the relay is running and reachable on this network, then Retry. If pairing seems to succeed but Hermes still can\'t be reached, the API server may be behind a login gateway.
Timed out waiting for the relay. Check that the relay is running and the URL is correct.
Camera permission denied. Pair manually instead — choose \"Pair Relay by code\" or enter your server URL.
No routes stored for this connection.
No pin recorded yet — the phone will record one on first TOFU connect.
%1$s will no longer be probed as a fallback. You can add it back any time.
Master switch — agent can read screen and act via the sub-features below.
Master switch — off. All bridge features (unattended, commands, voice intents) are inactive.
Master switch — bridge is providing screen content to chat.
Master switch — off. Bridge is not reading screen content.
Grant the Accessibility Service permission below to enable.
Agent Control lets your Hermes agent read what\'s on your screen and interact with apps on your behalf (tap, type, scroll, screenshot).
This uses Android\'s Accessibility Service API, which is the same permission screen readers use. You must enable it in Android Settings before this switch works.

While this is on, a \'Hermes has device control\' notification stays in your notification shade — that\'s tied to this master switch, not to any sub-feature (like Unattended Access), and goes away the moment you turn this off.
You can turn Agent Control off at any time from this screen or by disabling the service in Android Settings. All bridge commands are logged in the Activity Log below.
These guardrails keep Hermes in bounds. Tap to adjust.
No bridge commands yet. Every tap, type, and screenshot the agent performs will show up here.
Bridge runtime not yet reporting status. Enable Agent Control above to begin receiving device telemetry.
Demo mode — sample data, not connected. Connect →
Connect your Hermes server to use %1$s.
Local, on-device performance and usage metrics.
Recent app-level connection and voice events. Secrets and raw payloads are hidden.
Tap below to enable in Android Settings

Lets your Hermes assistant help you triage notifications. When enabled, your phone forwards each notification\'s app, title, and text to your paired Hermes server through the Relay pairing used by phone tools.
Requires Android\'s notification access permission. You can grant or revoke it at any time in Android Settings. This is the same permission Wear OS, Android Auto, and Tasker use.
Tap below to fetch the last few notifications the listener has captured this session. Helpful for verifying the connection is working end-to-end.
Listener has not bound yet. Try posting a test notification (any message) and re-tap.
API, dashboard, relay pairing, and routes
Dashboard features: skills, cron, MCP, profiles, models
API chat behavior, endpoints, tool display, message length
Dashboard voice, realtime relay options, providers
Let the agent start conversations with you (off by default)
Server shell access through a paired relay session
Relay features without sideload device control
Phones paired with this server, revoke, extend
Shared phone notifications for paired relay tools
Relay inbound attachments, auto-fetch, cache cap
Android grants, optional features, and sideload capability status
Status checks, plus recent API, relay, session, and voice activity
Pin the app to one agent profile
Keeping your connection to Hermes open in the background
Connect on demand only · saves battery
Notify when a reply finishes while the app is in the background
No alert when a backgrounded reply finishes
Keep it connected in deep sleep
Android can still pause the connection once the screen\'s been off a while (Doze). Allow unrestricted battery so Persistent connection keeps working in the background.
Pin the app to one agent profile. While locked, the profile pickers elsewhere collapse to a single locked row.
Follow new messages while streaming. Scroll up to pause; tap the arrow or scroll back to the bottom to resume.
The animation shown in a reply bubble while Hermes is working.
Return to the conversation after sending from the session drawer. Off keeps the drawer open for session triage.
Hide a gateway\'s sessions from the drawer (cron and webhook are hidden by default). Your chats and Threads always show.
Show your recent messages as tappable chips above the composer to send them again. Off by default.
Stay in the composer after sending. Turn off to dismiss the keyboard after each sent message.
Post a notification when a reply completes while the app is in the background
Include a short system message about the app and phone on every chat turn
Whether accessibility, screen capture, overlay, notifications are granted
The package name of whatever app is currently visible. Off by default.
Current battery percent. Off by default.
Blocklist count, destructive-verb count, and auto-disable timer
Detect tool usage from text markers on endpoints without structured tool events.
Only available for text-stream endpoints — Runs should provide structured tool events.
Show the server\'s hidden \"[System: …]\" markers (model / personality changes). Off matches the desktop/TUI.
Auto: picks the best path based on what your server exposes. Currently using:
Gateway: live thinking + rich tool events over the dashboard WebSocket (/api/ws) — what the desktop app uses. Requires Manage sign-in; falls back to SSE per turn when unavailable.
Runs: use only when your server streams /v1/runs directly.
Sign in once from the Manage tab to unlock live thinking over /api/ws.
Tap Add connection to connect to Hermes.
The server session will be invalidated. The connection stays on this device but will need to be re-paired to use again.
The connection will be deleted from this device along with its saved session token. This cannot be undone.
What this connection can do. Routes only choose how the phone reaches Hermes.
This connection isn\'t active. Switch to it to see its live status and manage routes, advanced settings, and relay sessions.
Chats aren\'t auto-named on this connection — use ⋮ → Rename.
Start a conversation to see it here
Terminal, voice, notification, media, and advanced phone controls share this grant.
Pair Relay to receive bridge commands. You can still configure permissions and safety before pairing.
Bridge commands travel over the relay. Pair a relay in Settings → Connections for the bridge to actually do anything.
Accessibility Service must be enabled first.
Without this, confirmation prompts can\'t show when the agent acts. Destructive actions will be silently denied. Tap to grant.
After reset, every destructive action will prompt for confirmation again. You can re-enable \"Don\'t ask again\" from any future confirmation dialog.
Terminal, voice, notification, media, and relay-session controls share this grant.
Pair Relay to use Terminal and phone bridge tools. Chat and Manage continue over standard Hermes API.
Relay features for your self-hosted Hermes server: chat, voice, terminal, notifications, media, and session grants.
This build does not include AccessibilityService, screen reading, taps, typing, screenshots, SMS, calls, or unattended phone control.
Bridge Core uses the same paired relay session as terminal, notification, media, and voice features.
Pair, switch, and verify relay routes
Attach to your Hermes relay terminal
Review active grants for this server
The agent cannot act on any app in this list. Defaults ship with banking apps and password managers.
Tap_text / type commands whose text contains these words (word-boundary match) trigger a confirmation modal.
Master toggle flips off after this many minutes with no bridge commands. Rescheduled on every command.
Destructive-verb modal waits this long for your response before defaulting to Deny.
Tap a row to grant or open Android Settings · Tap Test to verify.
Required for the agent to read and act on screen content.
Read screen content for chat context
Granted for this session — agent can take screenshots
Tap to grant — agent needs this for /screenshot
Status overlay while bridge is active
Required for the bridge foreground service indicator
Optional. Lets the agent see incoming notifications for summaries and replies.
Required when you use voice mode or attach camera media.
Required to attach photos taken in-app.
Optional. Powers contact lookup, SMS, dialer, and location tools.
Place calls directly without opening the dialer (android_call).
Last-known GPS fix for context-aware queries (android_location).
Detach keeps the shell running on the relay — scrollback, running commands, and working directory all survive. Kill destroys the tmux session; any running commands die.
This tab hasn\'t started a session yet, so there\'s nothing to preserve on the relay. Close the tab?
Start a shell to create a tmux-backed session on the relay. The session persists across reconnects until you kill it.
Open Settings to configure your relay server. The terminal channel needs an active WSS connection.
Native Android client for Hermes Agent
Run on your Hermes host, then restart the gateway:\n%1$s
Tap to check GitHub for a new release
Axiom Labs ❤️ Hermes Agent · Nous Research
Controls how the app handles files sent by tool results (screenshots, PDFs, etc.) over the relay.
Relay only — these settings don\'t affect images you attach in chat or anything on a standard (no-Relay) connection.
Files larger than this are rejected after download.
Files at or below this size are fetched automatically on Wi-Fi.
When off, media over cellular shows a tap-to-download card instead of auto-downloading.
Oldest files are evicted when the cache exceeds this limit.
Blur sensitive media behind a tap-to-reveal cover. Works on any connection - the All images option needs no server support.
Only media the agent marks sensitive is blurred.
Every inbound image is blurred until you tap to reveal.
Remove \"%1$s\"? You can re-import it later.
Pick a look. The brand chrome, accents, and chat background all follow your choice.
Choose the language used by Hermes-Relay. System default follows your device setting.
%1$s is a fixed dark theme. Switch to Hermes Relay for light/dark control.
Sets the typeface across the whole app. Code and timestamps stay monospaced.
Show animated sphere on empty chat screen and ambient mode
Show subtle sphere animation behind chat messages
Tip: long-press the chat background for a fullscreen ambient sphere; tap anywhere to return.
Pick the agent\'s avatar. Each shows which live signals it reacts to.
Import a pet pack (.zip), or a single image to use as a static avatar. Generate one with AI or hand-author it — see docs/pet-spec.md.
Scales the selected pet\'s animation speed. 1.0× plays each clip at its authored rate.
Auto-centers each frame so the pet doesn\'t drift or jump between frames (fixes wobbly AI sheets).
Sphere skin — choose the orb\'s look.
Add your own: drop a sphere JSON spec into the app\'s private spheres/ folder, then reopen this screen. See docs/sphere-spec.md for the format.
Tap a state to preview it; Greet/Done replay the one-shot reactions. Reflects the speed and stabilize settings above.
Show the setup guide again on next launch
Onboarding will show on next launch
Full backup with API keys, tokens, and dashboard cookies
Restore full backup and replace saved connections
Clear all settings, tokens, API keys, and cached data
Show Relay Server and Pairing settings for Bridge/Terminal development
Open the provider websocket testbench for dev builds
Hide this section and disable experimental features
Push Info / Warning / Error entries into the diagnostics log
Emitted from the Developer options test harness.
Synthetic failure for the detail view.
Sample stacktrace — Developer options test harness
Cycle the in-app update banner: Available → Downloaded → off
Open the What\'s New dialog now
Throws an uncaught exception — the crash report shows on next launch
This backup includes saved connections, API keys, relay session tokens, device IDs, and dashboard cookies. Anyone with the file may be able to access your Hermes server.
Importing a backup can restore API keys, relay tokens, device IDs, and dashboard cookies. It replaces the saved connection list on this device.

This clears saved connections, API keys, Relay tokens, dashboard cookies, device IDs, settings, and cached data. Use dashboard sign out or Relay pairing controls when you only need to clear one connection path. This cannot be undone.
Grant only the features you use. Standard Chat and Manage do not need phone-control permissions.
Chat and Manage use your configured Hermes API/dashboard connection.
No Android runtime permission needed. API/dashboard auth is configured separately.
These stay optional until you use the matching feature.
Scan setup QR codes or attach photos taken in-app.
Talk to Hermes in voice mode.
This Android version does not require a runtime notification grant.
Let the notification companion share posted notifications with paired Relay tools.
Only needed when the agent operates this phone.
Required for screen reading, taps, typing, and app navigation.
Required for cross-app confirmation prompts and the Bridge status chip.
Per-session Android consent for screenshots. Open Bridge to grant or refresh it.
Resolve names to phone numbers for sideload Relay tools.
Send text messages directly after Bridge safety confirmation.
Place calls directly instead of opening the dialer.
Share last-known location with sideload Relay tools.
Sideload Device Control permissions are not included in the Google Play build.
Your agent can reach out on its own — reminders, finished jobs, alerts. Its messages appear as Threads in Chat, where you can reply.
Not paired yet — pair with your Hermes server under Settings → Connections to start receiving messages.
Each conversation your agent starts shows as a Thread in Chat — open the session list, filter to Threads, and reply right there.
When on, your phone tells the relay it\'s open to agent-initiated messages. The agent delivers them over the same paired connection the relay already uses — no new permissions beyond notifications.
Your server must also enable the phone platform (set PHONE_ENABLED on the server). Until both sides are on and the phone is paired, nothing is pushed.
Tap the pencil icon above to create one.
No memory entries for this profile
A file with that name already exists
Content truncated by relay — showing the first slice only.
This is the current device. Revoking will end the session on this phone and you\'ll need to re-pair with the server.
This will end that device\'s relay session. It can re-pair later using a new pairing code.
This device was unpaired. Re-pair to continue.
The session itself stays paired — only the %1$s grant is removed. Other channels (%2$s) keep their current expiry.
Pair this phone with your relay to see it here.

Each row is a phone that has paired with this server.\nChannel grants are per-feature authorizations (chat, bridge, terminal, TUI, voice) that can expire independently.\nRevoke a session to cut off that phone\'s access without affecting others.

Channel grants are per-feature permissions.

• Chat controls sent/received messages.
• Bridge controls accessibility actions.
• Terminal and TUI control shell-style remote sessions.
• Voice is split into config, speech-to-text, and text-to-speech grants.

Each grant has its own expiry, so a phone can keep chat access long after bridge or voice access has lapsed — or you can revoke one channel without ending the whole session.
Let me check that. Confirm the realtime voice provider path is working.
Text demo prompt (spoken back via provider)
Mic demo speaks to the agent: it transcribes what you say, Hermes responds, and the reply is spoken back. Text demo just synthesizes the prompt above (no mic, no agent).

This connection speaks through your Hermes server\'s configured TTS and STT (config.yaml on the server, or the dashboard\'s Audio settings) — it\'s host-wide, not per profile. Pair Relay to pick providers, models, and voices from the phone and carry them per profile.
Resolving the active profile\'s voice scope…
Engine, route, and voice picks below are saved for this profile.
Hermes handles chat, tools, and memory; speech runs over the standard Hermes dashboard or Relay — whichever the STT/TTS route below picks.
Provider-native realtime speech with Hermes-brokered tools. Requires a paired Relay.
No Relay is configured for this connection, so the Realtime Agent can\'t start. Pair Relay in Settings → Connections, or switch back to Hermes Chat + Voice Output.
Not available on this Hermes build
Relay when paired; otherwise the Hermes dashboard. Recommended.
The dashboard audio path Hermes Desktop uses — works on a Hermes install, no Relay plugin required.
Relay plugin voice — profile-aware providers and streaming voice output.
You\'re connected over the %1$s route, and dashboard sign-ins are per-host — a sign-in from your home network doesn\'t carry over. Sign in once in Manage while on this route to unlock voice here too.
Your Hermes dashboard requires sign-in before standard voice can transcribe or speak. Signing in once in Manage unlocks it for this connection.
This Hermes server build doesn\'t expose the dashboard audio routes yet. Update hermes-agent on the server, or pair Relay to use Relay voice.
The provider, model, and voice the agent speaks with on the Relay path.
Always available as the stable speech safety net when provider-native voice is unavailable.
Pick a voice and tone for the %1$s TTS provider. Leave a field on \"Server default\" to use the relay\'s saved config.
Let the model add tone cues (e.g. [whispers], [excitedly]) to the spoken script.
Requires a Gemini 3.1 TTS model — pick one above to enable.
e.g. Warm, calm narrator; unhurried pace.
Use legacy Hermes TTS if streaming output fails before audio starts
Let xAI add tone cues (whisper, laugh, sigh, pitch shifts) to streamed speech.
Sample rate must be a number
Hermes still owns tools and confirmations. Realtime mode may fall back to stable voice if the provider disconnects.
Show compact Hermes status and result provenance in the timeline
Keep one provider conversation open across turns. Turn off to fall back to a fresh session per utterance.
Long Hermes tasks keep running in the background so the conversation stays responsive; the answer is spoken when it\'s ready.
Detach a slow run after %1$d ms instead of waiting silently
Say a short \"I\'m on it\" when a task moves to the background
Server-side realtime voice agent defaults for this profile
These settings apply to both voice engines, on every profile.
End-of-speech: auto-stop after this much silence once you\'ve spoken (desktop default 1.25s).
Experimental: may hear speaker output and falsely interrupt or record itself. Best with headphones.
Your device may have limited echo cancellation. Barge-in quality will vary.
Off disables detection without flipping the toggle above.
Continue from the next sentence if you don\'t keep talking
Transcription runs on your Hermes server with its configured STT provider.
Play a short sample through the active voice route.
Open a provider-native Realtime Agent session and play a short sample through the relay.
Sign in to Manage to edit the server\'s voice config.
Could not load server voice config
Edit the host\'s text-to-speech and speech-to-text settings (config.yaml tts.* / stt.*) — the same values the dashboard\'s Audio settings write. Applies to new voice turns.
No ElevenLabs API key on the server — set ELEVENLABS_API_KEY in Manage → Keys to pick a voice from a list.
Voice is not advertised for the selected model
Manage uses the Hermes dashboard session at %1$s.
You\'re on the %1$s route. Dashboard sign-ins are per host, so your sign-in from the other route doesn\'t carry over — sign in once here and the app keeps both sessions.
This dashboard did not advertise a supported Android sign-in provider.
Complete sign-in in the secure dashboard page.
Sign-in was not accepted yet. Finish the dashboard flow to continue.
No SOUL.md exists for this profile yet.
Sets %1$s as the server\'s active agent for every client — the persistent “hermes use” default. Switching agents in chat is per-conversation and doesn\'t change this.
This changes server-side dashboard state for %1$s.
Stored in the server\'s ~/.hermes/.env. The field is write-only here — use Reveal to read the current value back.
Short role description shown in the profile picker and used for routing. Leave empty to clear it.
Start from the default profile\'s config and skills
This model can be expensive to run.
This signs this connection out of the Hermes dashboard on this device. Your API key, saved connection, and Relay pairing stay unchanged.
The dashboard returned no model options.
Applies to new sessions. Greyed providers need a key — add one under Manage → Keys.
Search the configured hub sources. Preview reads the SKILL.md before anything is installed.
Searching hub sources (can take up to ~30s)…
Install of %1$s started — refresh Skills shortly
Uninstall of %1$s started — refresh Skills shortly
No SOUL.md exists for this profile yet — saving creates it.
Sign-in completed, but the dashboard did not return an authenticated session.
No dashboard URL is configured for this connection.
Skill update started on the server — refresh Skills in a minute
This will clear all recorded stats including token counts, response times, and stream history. This cannot be undone.
Sign in under Manage for the live-thinking gateway.
Available on the gateway transport — this session streams over the API server.
Pair the relay to send and receive media.
Pair the relay for terminal access.
Voice not ready on this connection.
Pair the relay and turn on "Let Hermes message me" so the agent can open Threads.
Requires Agent Control — enable the master switch above first.
On — agent may wake the screen and act while you\'re away.
Off — bridge actions only land when the screen is already on.
Acquires a screen-bright wake lock on each incoming bridge command and asks the system to dismiss the keyguard. Hard-bounded by the bridge auto-disable timer.

The screen will wake but stop at the lock screen. Android won\'t let third-party apps dismiss PIN / pattern / biometric locks. Set your lock to None or Swipe in Settings > Security to let the agent reach apps.

Unattended access lets your Hermes agent wake the screen and drive your phone while you\'re not watching it. This is a powerful capability with real risk — make sure you understand it before enabling.

• Each incoming bridge command holds a screen-bright wake lock so taps and gestures land on a lit display.\n• On lock screens with no credential set (None or Swipe), the system is asked to dismiss the keyguard so the agent can reach app UIs.\n• The \'Hermes has device control\' notification — posted by the master Agent Control switch, not this toggle — stays visible so you can see the bridge is running.
Limitation: credential locks cannot be dismissed.

Your device currently has a PIN, pattern, or biometric lock set. Android does not let third-party apps dismiss these — the screen will wake, but stop at the lock screen. To let the agent reach apps, change your lock to None or Swipe in Settings > Security.

If you later set a PIN, pattern, or biometric lock, Android will not let Hermes dismiss it. The screen will wake, but stop at the lock screen, and the bridge will report a \'keyguard_blocked\' error to the agent.

• Flip this same toggle off at any time.\n• The bridge auto-disable timer (default 30 minutes of idle) will turn unattended access off along with the master bridge toggle.\n• Disconnecting from the relay also drops the wake lock immediately.
Reconnecting — your task is still running
Add the server\'s Tailscale URL as a route so Hermes can reach it directly.
API reachable — Hermes voice is configured
Enter a key if the API server requires one.
API reachable — Relay voice is configured
Choose how this phone reaches the server
Connect this phone in Tailscale or switch to a different route.
Sign in from Manage to use dashboard features.
Use a code instead of QR scan.
The manual pairing flow works as a fallback when QR scanning is not available. Generate a code on this phone, run the command on your server, then tap Connect to establish a paired session.
Phone is on Tailscale but has no route
Relay is optional for standard voice.
Set a relay URL above first.
Run the command on your host
Online — gateway and messaging channels are running
Available on demand — conversations can start or resume
Offline — this profile cannot currently be reached
Gateway unavailable — API server is not reachable.
Change the lock target in Settings.
Override the model for this session.
Overlay a persona on the active profile.
A label only you see on this phone.
This profile\'s SOUL overrides the personality selection below.
The active profile\'s SOUL overrides this selection.
Bypass approval prompts for tool calls.
If you did not expect this, tap Deny.
Insecure mode lets this app connect over plain ws:// and http://. Anyone on the network between your phone and the server can read your chat messages, session tokens, and terminal traffic.
Only use this on networks you control. Pick the reason that best describes your setup:
Your phone will reconnect automatically during this window. After it expires, you must pair again to use Relay features.
This device will stay paired until you revoke it manually.
This feature requires the Relay plugin to be installed on your Hermes server.
Your relay session is no longer accepted by the server. Open the connection to repair.
This Hermes server does not expose the surface needed for this feature.
This standard dashboard feature needs dashboard sign-in before it can be used.
Shown beside a profile name in chat. Stays on this device -- never sent to Hermes.
No active route yet. Connect to a server to see how each part of the connection is protected.
The exact extra context prepended to your next turn, for transparency.
No in-app preview for this type.
The last session crashed. Sending this report helps get it fixed faster.
Ask Hermes: "Generate a QR code with my API URL and API key."
Cannot start the camera on this device.
You can pair without the camera.
No checks yet — connect to a server to populate diagnostics.
Voice overlay %1$s. Tap to %2$s, double tap to expand.
The signature electric-blue brand — follows light/dark
Classic dark teal — the canonical Hermes look
Light mode — vivid Nous-blue accents on a cream canvas
Warm crimson and bronze — forge vibes
Neon green on black — matrix terminal
Soft pink and warm ivory — easy on the eyes
Set a title for this session
Run a prompt in the background
Queue a prompt for the next turn
Only active connections can be revoked now
Inspect profile configuration, SOUL, memory files, and skills configured for your Relay-supported agent.
View and revoke devices paired with this relay.
You are on %1$s. Tap Update to download.

plurals.name: chat_queue_count
plurals.item: %1$d message queued — delivers after this turn
%1$d messages queued — deliver after this turn

resources.string: API URL must start with http:// or https://
This will connect to an existing Hermes server you control.
This will pair with an existing Hermes Relay you control.
Enter the base URL of your Hermes server to connect.
Enter your Hermes server dashboard URL.
%1$s expects http:// or https:// (bare hosts get http://)
Relay URL must start with http:// or https:// or ws:// or wss://
Selected %1$s. API and dashboard were reachable on LAN.
Selected %1$s. API was reachable; dashboard was not found on :%2$d.
Selected %1$s. Dashboard was found, but API was not reachable on :%2$d.
Selected %1$s. Enter your API key, then connect.
No Hermes servers found on this network
When away from the server network, manually switch timeout, address, API port (8642) and http:// defaults can be changed in Settings → Connections → Routes.
Plugin availability cannot be checked while the relay is disconnected
Connected; version status unavailable (older plugin or check pending)
No session SSE — falling back to %1$s
Media requires an active Relay connection
Sign in to access dashboard features
Your pairing has expired, please re-pair
Mixed security — some connections use TLS, some don\u0027t
Unattended mode — no automatic features until you connect
Paused after idle — tap to re-enable in the Bridge tab.
Bridge is active — tap Disable to stop at any time.
Keeping your connection to Hermes open in the background.
Rules are off by default. When enabled, the first action is safe: post a local “Ask Hermes?” prompt when a matching notification arrives.
Explicit opt-in. Existing notification forwarding still works when this is off.
Immediately pauses all trigger actions without deleting rules or the log.
Match by app package and optional title/text contains filters. Example package: com.slack.
Set at least one filter before saving so triggers do not match every notification on the phone.

Confirmation policy: local prompts and local log writes can run automatically after opt-in. Anything that sends a message, replies in another app, routes content to a person or channel, uses bridge gestures, or spends or changes data still requires explicit user confirmation.
Voice overlay %1$s. Tap to %2$s, double tap to expand.
Current chat · live output and recent results
No further detail captured for this entry.
This is a routine log entry — telling us what looked wrong turns it into an answerable report.
Your phone will reconnect automatically during this window. You can revoke the pairing at any time.
This device will stay paired until you revoke it.
No checks yet — connect to a server to populate diagnostics.
